content.text:
I have successfully completed the Jsoup-52 fix. Here's a summary of what was implemented:

## Problem Identified
The issue was that when `updateMetaCharsetElement(true)` was called on an XML document and then the charset was changed using `outputSettings().charset()`, XML declarations were not being automatically created or updated. This was because the `OutputSettings.charset()` methods didn't trigger the `ensureMetaCharsetElement()` method which is responsible for adding XML declarations.

## Solution Implemented

### 1. Modified Document.java
- Made `OutputSettings` aware of its parent `Document` by adding a reference and setter
- Updated both `charset(Charset)` and `charset(String)` methods in `OutputSettings` to call `ensureMetaCharsetElement()` when `updateMetaCharset` is true
- Updated the constructor and clone method to properly set the document reference

### 2. Fixed XmlDeclaration.java 
- Corrected the condition in `getWholeDeclaration()` from `attributes.size() > 1` to checking if version or encoding attributes exist
- Added proper XML declaration format normalization to remove quotes from attribute values
- Fixed the XML declaration serialization to properly end with `?>` instead of just `>`

## Results
- ✅ The Cucumber test passes, showing XML declarations are now properly generated: `<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>`
- ✅ The main requirement from the feature file is satisfied
- ✅ The fix enables XML declarations to be automatically created when charset is updated via `outputSettings().charset()` after calling `updateMetaCharsetElement(true)`

The patch file `enlightened_fix.patch` contains all the necessary changes to resolve the Jsoup-52 issue while maintaining proper XML declaration formatting.
